Member-only story

3 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

I recently had the opportunity to interview with Tessell for a Frontend Engineer role. The interview process was well-structured and focused on evaluating both frontend fundamentals and problem-solving abilities. Here’s a complete breakdown of my interview experience.
Round 1: HR Screening
The process began with an introductory HR discussion covering:

• Current and expected CTC

• Notice period

• Availability to join

• Previous experience

• Reason for looking for a change
It was a standard screening round before moving on to the technical interviews.
Round 2: JavaScript & React
This round primarily focused on JavaScript fundamentals and React concepts.
Topics Discussed

• JavaScript fundamentals

• React lifecycle methods

• State management

• Redux

• Context API

• Component architecture and reusability

• Performance optimization

• Memoization
